requirement.original: commands/spec-update.md continuation-spec mode never writes the <!-- spec-continuation-of: ${TASK_ID} --> marker required by commands/commit.md Step 7 Stage 3. Fix the producer.
requirement.clarified: Add an instruction to commands/spec-update.md continuation-spec mode directing the agent to write <!-- spec-continuation-of: <task-id> --> (with the resolved task-id substituted) exactly once per (task-id, spec file) pair, before the first newly appended section for that task-id run.
requirement.what: One-instruction addition to commands/spec-update.md continuation-spec mode: write the machine-readable marker before each new cycle block, idempotently, with the resolved task-id value
requirement.why: commands/commit.md Step 7 Stage 3 grep predicate now requires this exact marker; without it every Stage 3 run finds zero candidates and exits 0 silently, making spec-continuation inert for all future cycles

included: commands/spec-update.md — continuation-spec mode cycle-block appending instructions (lines 50-81)
excluded: commands/commit.md — consumer side already correct at line 164
commands/dev.md, commands/close.md, commands/dev-command.md — route through spec-update; single fix point
Existing spec files — no retroactive marker injection

success_criteria: After a spec-update continuation run for task-id T, the spec file contains <!-- spec-continuation-of: T --> with the resolved task-id value
grep -lF '<!-- spec-continuation-of: T -->' <spec_path> exits 0 (Stage 3 content predicate passes)
The marker does not appear in --temp mode output
The marker is written at most once per (task-id, spec file) pair
constraints: The marker string must be byte-identical to what commit.md line 164 greps for
The agent must substitute the actual task-id value; no literal placeholder may be written
The instruction must be idempotent: check for existing marker before writing

out_of_path_observed_but_not_touched: commands/commit.md:164 — consumer grep predicate (correct; reference only)
templates/overnight-spec.md — spec template (no marker needed here; spec-update writes it dynamically)

root_cause_analysis.symptom: Stage 3 of /commit Step 7 always returns empty candidate set and exits 0 silently; spec-continuation is inert
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: commands/spec-update.md continuation-spec mode instructs cycle-block appending across 8 sections but contains no instruction to write the machine-readable marker <!-- spec-continuation-of: <task-id> --> that commands/commit.md:164 now requires as the ONLY content predicate
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: aea75d9 — cycle 20260522-080646 tightened Stage 3 grep predicate in commit.md but did not update the producer
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Cycle C changed the consumer (commit.md) to use a machine-readable marker grep to eliminate false positives from prose references. The corresponding producer update was deferred and documented as W3 warning.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Zero specs carry the marker; Stage 3 always finds empty candidate set; the fail-closed outcome prints 'No spec associated with task-id' and exits 0, silently skipping spec-continuation for all future cycles
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Introduced in commit aea75d9 (2026-05-22); affects all /commit invocations since then
observations: grep -rF 'spec-continuation-of' docs/dev/specs/ → no matches in any of 4 existing spec files (inferred: source code read + grep confirmed)
commands/commit.md:164 requires grep -lF '<!-- spec-continuation-of: ${TASK_ID} -->' (inferred: file read)
commands/spec-update.md lines 50-81 contain no marker-writing instruction (inferred: file read)
docs/dev/close-report-20260522-080646.md:79 documents W3 warning acknowledging the gap (inferred: grep result)
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: incomplete (inferred only — no runtime reproduction; the symptom is structural: the instruction is absent from the file)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: grep -rF 'spec-continuation-of' docs/dev/specs/ returned no matches; commands/spec-update.md lines 50-81 read in full — no marker-writing instruction present
root_cause_analysis.located_source: commands/spec-update.md:50-81 (continuation-spec mode cycle-block appending section — the missing instruction location)

context.recent_commits: aea75d9 — cycle 20260522-000000 close-report (introduced the consumer-side marker requirement without updating producer)

development_approach.strategy: Add one instruction to commands/spec-update.md continuation-spec mode: before appending the first new cycle block for a given task-id, check whether <!-- spec-continuation-of: <task-id> --> is already present; if not, write it immediately before the first newly appended section heading
development_approach.validation_approach: Static grep checks: (1) grep -F 'spec-continuation-of' commands/spec-update.md returns a match inside ## Continuation-spec mode; (2) grep -F 'spec-continuation-of' in ## Temp-note mode section returns no match; (3) the instruction contains 'idempotent' or 'if absent' language and explicit substitution requirement

acceptance_criteria.given: commands/spec-update.md continuation-spec mode is invoked for active task-id T
acceptance_criteria.when: agent appends a new cycle block to a spec file at docs/dev/specs/spec-*.md
acceptance_criteria.then: the spec file contains the literal string <!-- spec-continuation-of: T --> where T is the resolved task-id value (not a placeholder)

acceptance_criteria.given: a spec file produced by spec-update continuation run for task-id T
acceptance_criteria.then: grep exits 0 and returns the spec path (content predicate passes)

acceptance_criteria.given: commands/spec-update.md instructs the agent to write the marker
acceptance_criteria.when: agent produces the marker in the spec file
acceptance_criteria.then: the spec file does NOT contain the literal placeholder forms <!-- spec-continuation-of: ${TASK_ID} --> or <!-- spec-continuation-of: <task-id> -->
spec-continuation-of text appears inside ## Continuation-spec mode section only; ## Temp-note mode section contains no spec-continuation-of text

acceptance_criteria.given: a spec file already containing <!-- spec-continuation-of: T --> for task-id T
acceptance_criteria.when: spec-update continuation mode is invoked again for the same task-id T on the same spec
acceptance_criteria.then: the spec file contains exactly one occurrence of <!-- spec-continuation-of: T -->

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 6 issues (5 with PROPOSED_FIX, 1 OBSERVATION_ONLY) and 1 observation. Key findings: marker cardinality underspecified (once-per-file wrong for multi-task specs); placeholder substitution must be explicit; AC2 only tests grep predicate not full Stage 3 candidacy; ACs should verify mode isolation; idempotency AC needed; 2 ACs insufficient for an instruction contract.

findings.issue: Marker cardinality/placement underspecified — 'first line of each new Cycle block' could create duplicates across multiple sections
findings.proposed_fix: Write exactly one marker per (task-id, spec file) if absent; place it before the first newly appended continuation subsection for that task, or line 1 for a newly created spec
findings.rationale: Reframed requirement as 'once per (task-id, spec file) idempotent'; placement before first new section heading

findings.issue: 'Once per file' is wrong for long-lived specs reused by multiple task-ids
findings.proposed_fix: Use 'once per active task-id per spec file', not once globally
findings.rationale: Requirements and ACs now specify 'once per (task-id, spec file) pair'

findings.issue: Placeholder substitution needs to be explicit in the instruction
findings.proposed_fix: Say the producer must substitute the active task-id value and must not write literal ${TASK_ID} or <task-id>. Add a negative AC for placeholder literals.
findings.rationale: Added explicit substitution requirement to Must Have and AC3 as negative AC

findings.issue: AC2 only proves the grep predicate, not full Stage 3 candidacy (basename + mtime also required by commit.md)
findings.proposed_fix: Rename AC2 to 'content predicate passes' or note basename/mtime are tested by commit.md separately
findings.rationale: AC2 now scoped to 'content predicate' with explicit note that basename pattern and mtime are enforced by commit.md separately

findings.issue: ACs do not verify the instruction landed only in continuation-spec mode
findings.proposed_fix: Add AC that commands/spec-update.md contains the marker instruction inside ## Continuation-spec mode and ## Temp-note mode contains no spec-continuation-of

findings.issue: Scope is mostly correct — other producers (dev.md, close.md, dev-command.md) route through /spec-update; commit.md inline dispatch follows spec-update.md exactly
findings.rationale: Confirms single-fix-point analysis; no action needed

findings.issue: 2 ACs are thin for an instruction contract where no executable code is changed
findings.proposed_fix: Add placement/cardinality, placeholder-negative, and temp-mode-negative ACs
findings.rationale: Expanded to 5 ACs: AC1 (marker written), AC2 (content predicate), AC3 (no literal placeholder), AC4 (mode isolation), AC5 (idempotent)

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 5 PROPOSED_FIX findings applied: cardinality reframed as once-per-(task-id,file); substitution requirement made explicit; AC2 scoped to content predicate only; AC4 added for mode isolation; AC5 added for idempotency; AC3 added as placeholder-negative; total ACs expanded from 2 to 5

_risk_level_doc: low — single-file instruction-only change to a command markdown file; no hooks, no scripts, no security boundary
